description_long: This object is a rectangular stone block with a slightly arched top and a rough, weathered surface. The front face features two recessed rectangular niches and a shallow central depression on the top, suggesting it may have served a functional or decorative purpose. The sides are rounded and show subtle linear grooves, while the back is plain with no significant features. The stone has a pale, off-white coloration with hints of reddish and brownish discoloration, likely from exposure to the elements. The base is flat and appears stable, suitable for placement on the ground. The overall form and wear patterns suggest it could be used as a barrier or boundary marker in an urban or historical setting.
description: A weathered, rectangular stone block with recessed niches on the front and a slightly arched, grooved top. The surface is rough and pale with reddish discoloration.
description_view_0: Front view showing two recessed rectangular niches and a shallow central depression on the top.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the rounded end and the arch of the top surface, with visible weathering.
description_view_2: Back view revealing a plain, unadorned surface with subtle grooves and a flat base.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with a rounded end and visible reddish discoloration at the base.
attribute_location_context: historic urban street or plaza as a boundary or obstacle marker
attribute_installation_method: set directly on compacted earth or pavement
attribute_weathering_pattern: Surface shows signs of weathering with reddish-brown discoloration and some erosion, especially near the base and edges.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: isolated but could be aligned with similar blocks to form a barrier
